title: Branching and Loops in ARM
summary: Implement conditionals and loops in ARM assembly using branch instructions and condition codes.
content_html:
Branching allows non-linear execution. ARM uses condition codes from the CPSR to decide whether to branch.

Compare Instruction

CMP R0, R1     ; Sets flags based on R0 - R1 (does not store result)
CMP R0, #5    ; Compare R0 with 5

Branch Instructions

B  label       ; Unconditional branch
BEQ label      ; Branch if Equal (Z flag set)
BNE label      ; Branch if Not Equal
BLT label      ; Branch if Less Than
BGT label      ; Branch if Greater Than
BLE label      ; Branch if Less or Equal
BGE label      ; Branch if Greater or Equal
BL  label      ; Branch with Link (function call, saves return in LR)

Example: Simple Loop (count from 0 to 9)

    MOV R0, #0        ; counter = 0
loop:
    ADD R0, R0, #1    ; counter++
    CMP R0, #10       ; compare with 10
    BLT loop          ; if R0 < 10, repeat

Function Calls

BL myFunc     ; Call myFunc, LR = next instruction
...
myFunc:
    ...code...
    MOV PC, LR  ; Return (or BX LR)

exercises.title: B instruction
exercises.prompt: What does the 'B label' instruction do?

exercises.title: BEQ condition
options: The Z (zero) flag is set (previous comparison was equal)
exercises.answer: The Z (zero) flag is set (previous comparison was equal)

exercises.title: CMP instruction
options: Subtracts R1 from R0 and sets flags, discards result
Compares and stores R0-R1 in R0
exercises.answer: Subtracts R1 from R0 and sets flags, discards result

exercises.title: BL instruction
exercises.prompt: What does 'BL myFunc' do that 'B myFunc' does not?
options: Saves the return address in LR (R14)
exercises.answer: Saves the return address in LR (R14)

exercises.title: Function return
exercises.prompt: To return from a function (BL call), you typically use:

exercises.title: BLT meaning
options: The comparison result was less than (signed)
Both flags N and Z are set
exercises.answer: The comparison result was less than (signed)

exercises.title: Loop counter
exercises.prompt: In a loop using 'CMP R0, #10' and 'BLT loop', the loop runs while:

exercises.title: LR register purpose
options: Return address after a BL function call
exercises.answer: Return address after a BL function call
